reforms.description: The government has proposed a draft law to improve the operation of capital markets, focusing on facilitating the supply of risk finance and easing criteria for company admission to the stock exchange, which aims to foster the flotation of new and smaller enterprises.
reforms.package_name: 50 Point Programme for Investment and Employment
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By easing access to capital for startups and small firms, the reform is expected to enhance entrepreneurial activity and innovation, contributing positively to economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The proposed reform is currently in the draft stage and has been circulated for comments.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant structural change in the capital market framework, aimed at enhancing the entrepreneurial climate and supporting small and medium-sized enterprises.
reforms.source_quote: The intention has now progressed to the stage of draft laws... which have now been circulated to interested parties for comment.

reforms.description: The government has proposed a gradual reduction in subsidies to the coal industry, with a plan to decrease federal subsidies from DM 8 billion in 1997 to DM 3.8 billion by 2005, aiming to reduce reliance on state support.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Reducing subsidies may lead to short-term economic challenges in the coal sector, potentially impacting employment and investment.
reforms.status_evidence: The proposal has been officially legislated but not yet implemented.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform represents a significant policy change in subsidy allocation but is not a structural overhaul.
reforms.source_quote: The government proposed a gradual reduction in subsidies to the coal industry, but in the face of industrial action and protests by affected Länder, the proposal was diluted.

reforms.description: The government has initiated a subsidised loan programme through state-owned banks aimed at supporting the construction sector, reflecting a targeted approach to stimulate economic activity in this area.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The subsidised loan programme is expected to stimulate economic activity and investment in the construction sector.
reforms.status_evidence: The programme has been initiated and is currently in operation.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform is significant in its targeted approach to support a specific sector but does not represent a major systemic change.
reforms.source_quote: The government has also initiated a subsidised loan programme through state-owned banks which favours the construction sector.

reforms.description: The major subsidy programmes for eastern Germany have been reviewed and extended until the end of 1998, with the aim of further upgrading infrastructure and broadening the industrial base in the region.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Extending subsidies is intended to enhance infrastructure and industrial development, which can positively impact regional economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The subsidy programmes have been reviewed and are currently in effect until the end of 1998.
reforms.importance_rationale: The extension of subsidy programmes is significant for regional development but does not constitute a major reform.
reforms.source_quote: The major subsidy programmes for eastern Germany... were prolonged until the end of 1998 pending a final review.
